title: Import package error
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.34+14 (git sha1 3e2279181, clang 10.0.0-4ubuntu1 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

Thanks for this tool!

I found an error with package imports. (Similar to #2187)

I have a package `"my_pkg.sv"`, and two tests `"test1.sv"` and `"test2.sv"`:

```systemverilog
package my_pkg;
    localparam NUM = 1;
endpackage
```
```systemverilog
module test1 import my_pkg::*; (
    input a,
    output b
);
    assign b = (a ^ NUM);
endmodule
```
```systemverilog
module test2 (
    input a,
    output b
);
    import my_pkg::*;
    assign b = (a ^ NUM);
endmodule
```

This is my tcl script:

```shell
# both these cause and error:
read_verilog -sv my_pkg.sv testX.sv
# or...
read_verilog -sv my_pkg.sv
read_verilog -sv testX.sv
```

### Expected Behavior

This is valid syntax for VCS, Icarus, and Verilator

### Actual Behavior

Both files give this error:

```log
Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: test2.sv
Parsing SystemVerilog input from `test2.sv' to AST representation.
test2.sv: ERROR: syntax error, unexpected TOK_PACKAGESEP, expecting '(' or '['
```
code_blocks:
Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: test2.sv
Parsing SystemVerilog input from `test2.sv' to AST representation.
test2.sv: ERROR: syntax error, unexpected TOK_PACKAGESEP, expecting '(' or '['
